Article Title: TVK goes solo in Puducherry elections, fields candidates in all constituencies
Article Text: PUDUCHERRY: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), the political party led by Tamil actor Vijay , has announced its candidates for the upcoming Puducherry Assembly elections and will contest all 30 constituencies independently.The list includes two women candidates, who have been fielded from Mangalam and Kadirkamam in the Puducherry region.According to a PTI report, A K Sai J Saravanan Kumar has been named as the party’s candidate from the Thirubhuvanai reserved constituency. He recently resigned from the BJP after being denied a ticket to recontest from the Oussudu reserved seat.The party has also inducted experienced leaders from other political backgrounds.Former AIADMK legislators K A U Asana and L Periyasamy will contest from Karaikal (South) and Nettapakkam (reserved), respectively. In addition, former Puducherry BJP president V Saminathan has been fielded from the Lawspet constituency.Other prominent candidates include former Deputy Inspector General of Police V J Chandran and former Indian National Congress legislator N Dhanavelou.The party has also announced nominees for the remaining constituencies, completing its full slate for the election.
Article Summary: The list includes two women candidates, who have been fielded from Mangalam and Kadirkamam in the Puducherry region. Former AIADMK legislators K A U Asana and L Periyasamy will contest from Karaikal (South) and Nettapakkam (reserved)

Article Title: Datta Meghe: Veteran BJP leader Datta Meghe passes away at 89; great loss for Vidarbha, says Gadkari
Article Text: NAGPUR: Veteran BJP leader Datta Meghe passed away on Sunday evening at the age of 89, his close aide and former Nagpur deputy mayor Raghunath Malikar said.Meghe is the father of Hingna MLA Sameer Meghe and former MLC Sagar Meghe.Datta Meghe was a four-time Congress Lok Sabha MP from Nagpur, Ramtek and Wardha and was also Rajya Sabha member between 2002 and 2008. He was part of the Sharad Pawar-led (undivided) NCP when it was formed in 1999 and was a state minister.He joined the Bharatiya Janata Party in 2014. He is survived by his wife, two sons, two daughters and grandchildren.Union Minister Nitin Gadkari said Meghe was a like an elder brother and the news of his passing away was heart-breaking."My heartfelt tribute to him. With Dattabhau's passing, the political and social spheres of Vidarbha have suffered a great loss. Such a leader with a generous heart, broad and pure mind, will not come again. Dattabhau's contributions to the educational and medical fields in Vidarbha are noteworthy and significant," he said.From Gadchiroli to Melghat, in the tribal areas, Meghe made utmost efforts to improve the health of tribal brethren through free medical camps, Gadkari said, adding that at Sawangi Meghe, he provided free medical treatment to the poor."To provide higher education opportunities to the youth of Vidarbha, he created educational institutions. May God grant peace to the departed soul and give strength to his family and everyone to recover from this grief," the Union minister said.
Article Summary: Datta Meghe was a four-time Congress Lok Sabha MP from Nagpur, Ramtek and Wardha. He was also Rajya Sabha member between 2002 and 2008. He is the father of Hingna MLA Sameer Meghe and former MLC Sagar Meghe.

Article Title: Drug peddler held with heroin worth Rs 1.5cr
Article Text:
.

JAMMU: Jammu Police arrested a drug peddler and seized from him around 262g heroin, worth approximately Rs 1.5 crore in the international market, along with Rs 1.5 lakh cash, a cellphone, and a weighing machine, on Saturday.A police naka team, led by PSI Rajan Choudhary, was deployed at Tutray Chowk Ring Road and spotted the suspect around 7.40pm. The accused tried to escape but was caught. Police identified him as Harpal Singh, a resident of Kathua district.Cops registered a case under NDPS Act at Miran Sahib PS. Further investigation was underway to unearth the forward and backward linkages of the nexus, police said.
Article Summary: Jammu Police arrested a drug peddler and seized from him around 262g heroin, worth approximately Rs 1.5 crore in the international market. A police naka team, led by PSI Rajan Choudhary, was deployed at Tutray Chowk Ring Road and spotted the suspect.

Article Title: Assam assembly elections: Denied ticket by BJP, minister Nandita Gorlosa joins Congress
Article Text: GUWAHATI: After being denied a BJP ticket for the upcoming assembly elections, Assam cabinet minister Nandita Gorlosa joined the Congress on Sunday night.She was serving as minister for sports and youth affairs and welfare of minorities. The BJP dropped Gorlosa from its candidate list for the Haflong seat, which she currently represents, and instead fielded first-time candidate Rupali Langthasa."Nandita Gorlosa has left BJP and joined Congress," Assam Congress media department chairman Bedabrat Bora said.Assam Congress also shared a photo showing Gorlosa joining the party. The induction reportedly took place in Haflong, while her comments on the matter are still awaited.Meanwhile, chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma visited Dima Hasao district on Sunday to wish Rupali well ahead of filing her nomination papers on Monday. Sarma said that 18 sitting MLAs were denied tickets and would be assigned different responsibilities after the election.
Article Summary: Nandita Gorlosa was serving as minister for sports and youth affairs and welfare of minorities. The BJP dropped her from its candidate list for the Haflong seat, which she currently represents. Chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma visited Dima Hasao district to wish Rupali well ahead of filing her nomination papers on Monday.
